flags.concern: Final-video badge suppression: Step 3's proposed `timelineGenerationIds` scan over `data.registry.assets` would treat any previously registered final-video asset as 'already on the timeline', even if no current clip is using it.
flags.evidence: `useSwitchToFinalVideo()` registers the final-video asset in the registry before editing rows (`src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts:54-57`), and row edits only delete clip meta / clip order while leaving registry entries intact (`src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineCommit.ts:193-249`). If Step 3 keys suppression off all registry assets, removing the final-video clip later would still leave `finalVideo.id` in the registry and hide the badge incorrectly.

flags.concern: Shot-group badge scope: Step 3 suppresses the reminder at shot level, but this repo can render multiple shot groups for the same shot, so switching one group to the final video would hide the badge on other groups of that shot too.
flags.evidence: `useShotGroups()` iterates every row and can flush/restart multiple groups for the same shot (`src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.ts:83-135`), while `TimelineCanvas` currently derives `hasFinalVideo` from `group.shotId` alone (`src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx:448-456`). The issue note calls for suppressing when a clip in 'the group' already uses the final video; Step 3's shot-level exclusion would over-suppress in multi-group timelines.
